key_insight: A PPO-based deep reinforcement learning policy learns when and where autonomous order pickers should charge, improving warehouse throughput under stochastic demand.
engineering_relevance: This can reduce queueing, cut recharging downtime, and support more efficient multi-robot warehouse operations with fixed charging stations.
method: Uses Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) for dynamic charging decisions.
Chooses both charging station and charging duration while accounting for predicted queue times.
Targets a multi-block warehouse with multiple AMRs and stochastic order arrivals.
evidence_signal: Numerical experiments across different warehouse configurations and arrival rates show up to a 6% higher order-completion rate than the strongest baseline, with lower total recharging time.
watch_item: Performance gains may depend on how accurately the environment and queueing dynamics are modeled, so real-world deployment will need careful validation and recalibration.
paper_title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Battery Management of Autonomous Order Pickers
